tool_input.summary: Relevant Mercury ring-current / storm-like magnetic depression studies in PDS are primarily supported by MESSENGER magnetometer (MAG) field time series and internal-field-model residual products (PDS4, PPI node), combined with MESSENGER EPPS plasma/energetic particle products (PDS4 + PDS3, PPI node) to estimate particle pressure/current strength. For geometry and coordinate transforms, MESSENGER SPICE kernels are available in PDS3 (NAIF node). No PDS3 MAG dataset surfaced in this catalog search, but PDS4 MAG bundles are available.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary magnetic-field observations at Mercury needed to look for storm-like depressions (Dst-like signatures) attributable to enhanced ring/tail currents; includes averaged products in multiple coordinate systems.

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides MAG residuals after subtracting the KT17 internal field model—useful for isolating external current systems (ring current, tail current, magnetopause) and quantifying any net depression relative to the internal dipole.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Highest-fidelity MAG measurements (including burst where available) if you need to reprocess/filter and examine rapid storm-time current enhancements and their magnetic signatures.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Ion plasma moments/derived products to estimate plasma pressure and infer whether a significant ring current could be supported during disturbed intervals; helps separate ring-current effects from purely boundary-current effects.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle environment for assessing trapped/energized populations that would contribute to a ring current and associated magnetic depression signatures.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 EPS DDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 predecessor of EPPS/EPS derived products (energetic particles) for ring-current proxy comparisons with MAG; useful if you are working in legacy PDS3 pipelines.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 FIPS DDR V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 predecessor of EPPS/FIPS derived plasma products to estimate particle pressure/current-strength during candidate storm-like depressions.

candidates.title: MESSENGER SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: Spacecraft ephemeris, pointing, and frames kernels needed to place MAG/EPPS observations in Mercury-centric coordinates (e.g., MLAT/MLT, MSO/MBF) when constructing a Mercury ‘Dst-like’ index or assessing current geometry.

You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Could Mercury's ring current produce a measurable magnetic storm like depression?

dataset.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
dataset.description: This data set consists of the MESSENGER MAG calibrated observations. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are five MAG data products, which mainly differ in the coordinate system. For each type the data are provided at full resolution, and one or more averaging intervals.

dataset.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known as DDRs. The system encompasses 2 instrument subsystems - the Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) and the Fast Imaging Plasma Spectrometer (FIPS).

dataset.title: MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains the MESSENGER MAG raw observations, also known as EDRs. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are eight MAG raw data products, including standard data, burst-mode data, and status and housekeeping data.

dataset.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) advanced data products, also known as DDR/DAPs. This bundle contains data from the Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) subsystem. EPS covers the energy range of 25 to > 500 keV for electrons, and 10 keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV total energy for ions. These data were previously released as a PDS data set (MESS-E/V/H/SW-EPPS-3-EPS-DDR-V1.0, https://doi.org/10.17189/1519741).

parts.args: {"summary":"Relevant Mercury ring-current / storm-like magnetic depression studies in PDS are primarily supported by MESSENGER magnetometer (MAG) field time series and internal-field-model residual products (PDS4, PPI node), combined with MESSENGER EPPS plasma/energetic particle products (PDS4 + PDS3, PPI node) to estimate particle pressure/current strength. For geometry and coordinate transforms, MESSENGER SPICE kernels are available in PDS3 (NAIF node). No PDS3 MAG dataset surfaced in this catalog search, but PDS4 MAG bundles are available.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-calibrated::1.0","title":"MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary magnetic-field observations at Mercury needed to look for storm-like depressions (Dst-like signatures) attributable to enhanced ring/tail currents; includes averaged products in multiple coordinate systems."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-kt17-model-residuals::1.0","title":"MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides MAG residuals after subtracting the KT17 internal field model—useful for isolating external current systems (ring current, tail current, magnetopause) and quantifying any net depression relative to the internal dipole."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-raw::1.0","title":"MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Highest-fidelity MAG measurements (including burst where available) if you need to reprocess/filter and examine rapid storm-time current enhancements and their magnetic signatures."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-fips-derived::1.2","title":"MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Ion plasma moments/derived products to estimate plasma pressure and infer whether a significant ring current could be supported during disturbed intervals; helps separate ring-current effects from purely boundary-current effects."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-eps-derived::1.1","title":"MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Energetic particle environment for assessing trapped/energized populations that would contribute to a ring current and associated magnetic depression signatures."},{"dataset_id":"MESS-E_V_H_SW-EPPS-3-EPS-DDR-V1.0","title":"MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 EPS DDR V1.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 predecessor of EPPS/EPS derived products (energetic particles) for ring-current proxy comparisons with MAG; useful if you are working in legacy PDS3 pipelines."},{"dataset_id":"MESS-E_V_H_SW-EPPS-3-FIPS-DDR-V2.0","title":"MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 FIPS DDR V2.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 predecessor of EPPS/FIPS derived plasma products to estimate particle pressure/current-strength during candidate storm-like depressions."},{"dataset_id":"MESS-E/V/H-SPICE-6-V1.0","title":"MESSENGER SPICE FILES","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"naif","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Spacecraft ephemeris, pointing, and frames kernels needed to place MAG/EPPS observations in Mercury-centric coordinates (e.g., MLAT/MLT, MSO/MBF) when constructing a Mercury ‘Dst-like’ index or assessing current geometry."}]}
